text.NRSV: Like a hunted gazelle, or like sheep with no one to gather them, all will face to their own people, and all will flee to their own land.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—Like a gazelle that is chased, And like a sheep with none to lift it up,—Each, to his own people, will they turn, And, each, to his own land, will they flee:

text.NRSV: Whoever is found will be thrust through, and whoever is caught will fall by the sword.
text.Rotherham: Every one found, shall be thrust through,—And, every one taken, shall fall by the sword;

text.NRSV: Their infants will be dashed to pieces before their eyes; their houses will be plundered, and their wives ravished.
text.Rotherham: And, their infants, shall be dashed to the ground, before their eyes,—Plundered shall be, their houses, and their wives ravished.

text.NRSV: See, I am stirring up the Medes against them, who have no regard for silver and do not delight in gold.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! stirring up against them the Medes,—Who of silver, shall take no account, And as for gold, they shall not delight in it;

text.NRSV: Their bows will slaughter the young men; they will have no mercy on the fruit of the womb; their eyes will not pity children.
text.Rotherham: And, bows, shall dash the young to pieces,—And on the fruit of the womb, will they have no pity, Over children, will their eye throw no shield.

text.NRSV: And Babylon, the glory of kingdoms, the splendor and pride of the Chaldeans, will be like Sodom and Gomorrah when God overthrew them.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall Babylon—The most lovely of kingdoms, The majestic beauty of the Chaldees—Become as in the divine overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah.

text.NRSV: It will never be inhabited or lived in for all generations; untilled by Arabs, shepherds will not make their flocks lie down there.
text.Rotherham: It shall not be dwelt in for ever, Neither shall it be inhabited from generation to generation,—Neither shall encamp there, an Arab, Nor, shepherds, fold their flocks there.

text.NRSV: But wild animals will lie down there, and its houses will be full of howling creatures; there ostriches will live, and there goat-demons will dance.
text.Rotherham: Then shall lie down there, wild beasts, And filled shall be their houses with owls,—Then shall inhabit there, the ostrich, And shaggy creatures, shall dance there.

text.NRSV: Hyenas will cry in its towers, and jackals in the pleasant palaces; its time is close at hand, and its days will not be prolonged.
text.Rotherham: And jackals shall answer, in their citadels, And wild dogs, in their voluptuous palaces,—And near to come is her time, And, her days, shall not be delayed.

text.NRSV: But the Lord will have compassion on Jacob and will again choose Israel, and will settle them in their own land; and aliens will join them and attach themselves to the house of Jacob.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh will have compassion on Jacob, And will yet choose Israel, And will give them rest upon their own soil,—And the sojourner, shall join himself, unto them, And they shall attach themselves unto the house of Jacob;

text.NRSV: And the nations will take them and bring them to their place, and the house of Israel will possess them as male and female slaves in the Lord’s land; they will take captive those who were their captors, and rule over those who oppressed them.
text.Rotherham: And peoples shall take them, and bring them into their own place, And the house of Israel shall possess themselves of them upon the soil of Yahweh for servants and for handmaids,—Thus shall they be taking captive their captors, And shall tread down their oppressors.

text.NRSV: When the Lord has given you rest from your pain and turmoil and the hard service with which you were made to serve,
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in the day when Yahweh shall give thee rest from thy toil, and from thy disquiet,—and from the hard service which had been laid upon thee,

text.NRSV: you will take up this taunt against the king of Babylon: How the oppressor has ceased! How his insolence has ceased!
text.Rotherham: that thou shalt take up this taunt over the king of Babylon, and shalt say:—How hath ceased the oppressor! Ceased the exactress!

text.NRSV: The Lord has broken the staff of the wicked, the scepter of rulers,
text.Rotherham: Broke hath Yahweh the staff of the lawless,—The sceptre of despots;

text.NRSV: that struck down the peoples in wrath with unceasing blows, that ruled the nations in anger with unrelenting persecution.
text.Rotherham: Smiting peoples in passion With stroke unremitting,—Trampling, in anger, on nations, Persecution unhindered.

text.NRSV: The whole earth is at rest and quiet; they break forth into singing.
text.Rotherham: Quiet, at rest, the whole earth,—Men have burst into shouting!

text.NRSV: The cypresses exult over you, the cedars of Lebanon, saying, “Since you were laid low, no logger comes up against us.”
text.Rotherham: Yea the pine-trees, make mirth at thee, Cedars of Lebanon, Since thou wast laid low, No feller hath come up against us!

text.NRSV: Sheol beneath is stirred up to meet you when you come; it rouses the shades to greet you, all who were leaders of the earth; it raises from their thrones all who were kings of the nations.
text.Rotherham: Hades beneath, is excited about thee To meet thine arrival,—Rousing up, for thee Shades, All the he-goats of earth! Maketh rise from their thrones, All the kings of the nations.

text.NRSV: All of them will speak and say to you: “You too have become as weak as we! You have become like us!”
text.Rotherham: All of them, answer, and say to thee,—Thou too, made strengthless, as we! Unto us, art thou like!

text.NRSV: Your pomp is brought down to Sheol, and the sound of your harps; maggots are the bed beneath you, and worms are your covering.
text.Rotherham: Brought down to Hades, thy pride. The hum of thy harps, Beneath thee, is spread out corruption, And, thy coverlet—worms!

text.NRSV: How you are fallen from heaven, O Day Star, son of Dawn! How you are cut down to the ground, you who laid the nations low!
text.Rotherham: How hast thou fallen from heaven, O Shining One—Son of the Dawn! Hewn down to the earth, O crusher of nations!

text.NRSV: You said in your heart, “I will ascend to heaven; I will raise my throne above the stars of God; I will sit on the mount of assembly on the heights of Zaphon;
text.Rotherham: Yet, thou, didst say in thy heart—The heavens, will I ascend, Above the stars of GOD, will I lift up my throne,—That I may sit in the Mount of Assembly In the Recesses of the North:

text.NRSV: I will ascend above the heights of the clouds, I will make myself like the Most High.”
text.Rotherham: I will mount on the hills of the clouds, I will match the Most High!

text.NRSV: But you are brought down to Sheol, to the depths of the Pit.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, to Hades, shalt thou be brought down,—To the Recesses of the Pit!

text.NRSV: Those who see you will stare at you, and ponder over you: “Is this the man who made the earth tremble, who shook kingdoms,
text.Rotherham: They who see thee, upon thee, will gaze, Upon thee, will thoughtfully muse,—Is this the man who startled the earth? Who terrified kingdoms?

text.NRSV: who made the world like a desert and overthrew its cities, who would not let his prisoners go home?”
text.Rotherham: Who made the world like a desert? And its cities, brake down? Its prisoners, he loosed not, Each one to his home?

text.NRSV: All the kings of the nations lie in glory, each in his own tomb;
text.Rotherham: All the kings of the nations—they all, are lying in state Each one in his crypt;

text.NRSV: but you are cast out, away from your grave, like loathsome carrion, clothed with the slain, those pierced by the sword, who go down to the stones of the Pit, like a corpse trampled underfoot.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, art flung out from thy grave, Like a scion detested, Beshrouded with slain, the pierced of the sword, Like a carcase trod underfoot:

text.NRSV: You will not be joined with them in burial, because you have destroyed your land, you have killed your people. May the offspring of evildoers nevermore be named!
text.Rotherham: As for them who go down to the stones of the Pit, Thou shalt not be united with them in burial; For thy land, thou didst ruin Thy people, didst slay,—Unnamed to times age-abiding, Be the seed of the wicked!

text.NRSV: Prepare slaughter for his sons because of the guilt of their father. Do not let them rise and possess the earth, and fill the face of the world with cities.
text.Rotherham: Prepare—for his sons—shambles. For the iniquity of their fathers,—They shall not rise nor possess the earth, Nor fill the face of the world with cities.

text.NRSV: I will rise up against them, says the Lord of hosts, and will cut off from Babylon name and remnant, offspring and posterity, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: I will rise. Then. against them, Declareth Yahweh of hosts,—And will cut off from Babylon Record and remnant And scion and seed, Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: And I will make it a possession of the hedgehog, and pools of water, and I will sweep it with the broom of destruction, says the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: And will make her a possession for the bittern, And marshes of water,—And will sweep it with the besom of destruction, Declareth Yahweh of hosts.

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts has sworn: As I have designed, so shall it be; and as I have planned, so shall it stand:
text.Rotherham: Yahweh of hosts, hath sworn, saying,—Surely as I intended, so, hath it come to pass, And as I purposed, the same, shall stand:—

text.NRSV: I will break the Assyrian in my land, and on my mountains trample him underfoot; his yoke shall be removed from them, and his burden from their shoulders.
text.Rotherham: That I will break in pieces the Assyrian in my land, Yea upon my mountains, will I tread him underfoot,—So shall be removed from off them his yoke, And his burden, from off their shoulder shall be removed.

text.NRSV: This is the plan that is planned concerning the whole earth; and this is the hand that is stretched out over all the nations.
text.Rotherham: This, is the purpose that is purposed upon all the earth,—And, this, the hand outstretched over all the nations;

text.NRSV: For the Lord of hosts has planned, and who will annul it? His hand is stretched out, and who will turn it back?
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh of hosts, hath purposed,—And who shall frustrate? And, his, is the hand outstretched, And who shall turn it back?

text.NRSV: In the year that King Ahaz died this oracle came:
text.Rotherham: In the year that King Ahaz died, came this oracle:—

text.NRSV: Do not rejoice, all you Philistines, that the rod that struck you is broken, for from the snake’s root will come forth an adder, and its fruit will be a flying fiery serpent.
text.Rotherham: Do not rejoice, Philistia, any of thee, In that the rod of him that smote thee, is broken,—For, out of the root of the serpent, shall come forth, a viper, And his fruit be a fiery dragon that flieth.

text.NRSV: The firstborn of the poor will graze, and the needy lie down in safety; but I will make your root die of famine, and your remnant I will kill.
text.Rotherham: Then shall feed the firstborn of the poor, And the needy, in confidence lie down,—And I will kill with famine thy root, And thy residue, shall be slain.

text.NRSV: Wail, O gate; cry, O city; melt in fear, O Philistia, all of you! For smoke comes out of the north, and there is no straggler in its ranks.
text.Rotherham: Howl—O gate, Make outcry—O city, Dispersed art thou Philistia, all of thee,—For out of the north, a smoke cometh in, With no straggler in his ranks.

text.NRSV: What will one answer the messengers of the nation? “The Lord has founded Zion, and the needy among his people will find refuge in her.”
text.Rotherham: What, then, shall one answer the messengers of a nation? That Yahweh, hath founded Zion, And in her, shall seek refuge the oppressed of his people.

text.NRSV: An oracle concerning Moab. Because Ar of Moab is laid waste in a night, Moab is undone; because Kir of Moab is laid waste in a night, Moab is undone.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Moab: Because, in a night, was laid waste Ar of Moab—destroyed! Because, in a night, was laid waste Kir of Moab—destroyed,

text.NRSV: Dibon has gone up to the temple, to the high places to weep; over Nebo and over Medeba Moab wails. On every head is baldness, every beard is shorn;
text.Rotherham: He hath gone up to Bayith and Dibon, to the high places, to weep,—On Nebo and on Medeba, Moab is howling, On all their heads, a baldness, Every beard, clipped.

text.NRSV: in the streets they bind on sackcloth; on the housetops and in the squares everyone wails and melts in tears.
text.Rotherham: In their streets, have they girded them with sackcloth,—On their housetops, and in their broadways, every one is howling—melting in tears;

text.NRSV: Heshbon and Elealeh cry out as far as Jahaz their voice is heard; therefore the loins of Moab quiver; his soul trembles.
text.Rotherham: And Heshbon, hath made outcry, and Elealeh, Unto Jahaz, hath been heard their voice,—For this cause, do the armed men of Moab roar, Every man’s soul, quivereth to him.

text.NRSV: My heart cries out for Moab; his fugitives flee to Zoar, to Eglath-shelishiyah. For at the ascent of Luhith they go up weeping; on the road to Horonaim they raise a cry of destruction;
text.Rotherham: Mine own heart, for Moab continueth to make outcry, Her fugitive, as far as Zoar, is like a heifer of three years; For the accent of Luhith, with weeping, they ascend, For by the way of Horonaim—an outcry of destruction, they excite;

text.NRSV: the waters of Nimrim are a desolation; the grass is withered, the new growth fails, the verdure is no more.
text.Rotherham: For, the waters of Nimrim, shall become desolation,—For grass, hath dried up, Herbage hath failed, Green thing, hath not sprung up!

text.NRSV: Therefore the abundance they have acquired and what they have laid up they carry away over the Wadi of the Willows.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, the savings they had made and that which they had stored, Over the torrent-bed of the willows, shall they bear them.

text.NRSV: For a cry has gone around the land of Moab; the wailing reaches to Eglaim, the wailing reaches to Beer-elim.
text.Rotherham: For the outcry hath gone round the boundary of Moab,—As far as Eglaim, the howling thereof, And to Beer-elim, the howling thereof.

text.NRSV: For the waters of Dimon are full of blood; yet I will bring upon Dimon even more— a lion for those of Moab who escape, for the remnant of the land.
text.Rotherham: For the waters of Dimon, are full of blood, For I will lay upon Dimon new troubles,—To the escaped of Moab, the lions, Even to the survivors on the soil.

text.NRSV: Send lambs to the ruler of the land, from Sela, by way of the desert, to the mount of daughter Zion.
text.Rotherham: Send ye the lamb due to the ruler of the land, From Sela towards the desert,—Unto the mount of the daughter of Zion;

text.NRSV: Like fluttering birds, like scattered nestlings, so are the daughters of Moab at the fords of the Arnon.
text.Rotherham: Yet shall it be that—Like wandering birds from a nest cast forth, Shall be the daughters of Moab, at the fords of Arnon.

text.NRSV: “Give counsel, grant justice; make your shade like night at the height of noon; hide the outcasts, do not betray the fugitive;
text.Rotherham: Bring thou, in counsel, Execute thou judgment, Make as the night, thy shadow in the midst of high noon,—Hide thou the outcasts, The wanderer, do not thou reveal.

text.NRSV: let the outcasts of Moab settle among you; be a refuge to them from the destroyer.” When the oppressor is no more, and destruction has ceased, and marauders have vanished from the land,
text.Rotherham: Let mine own outcasts, sojourn with thee, O Moab, become thou a covert to them from the face of the spoiler,—For vanished is the oppressor, At an end is extortion, They who tread down have ceased out of the land.

text.NRSV: then a throne shall be established in steadfast love in the tent of David, and on it shall sit in faithfulness a ruler who seeks justice and is swift to do what is right.
text.Rotherham: So shall be established, in lovingkindness, a throne, And one shall sit thereon, in truth, in the home of David,—Judging—and seeking justice and speeding righteousness.

text.NRSV: We have heard of the pride of Moab —how proud he is!— of his arrogance, his pride, and his insolence; his idle boasting is false.
text.Rotherham: We have heard of the arrogance of Moab, Proud exceedingly! His haughtiness and his arrogance and his passion, Not true, are his boastings.

text.NRSV: Therefore let Moab wail, let everyone wail for Moab; mourn, utterly stricken, for the raisin-cakes of Kir-hareseth.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall, Moab, howl for, Moab, All that belong to her, shall wail,—For the ruins of Kir-hareseth, shall they moan out Utterly stricken!

text.NRSV: For the fields of Heshbon languish, and the vines of Sibmah, whose clusters once made drunk the lords of the nations, reached to Jazer and strayed to the desert; their shoots spread abroad and passed over the sea.
text.Rotherham: For, the fields of Heshbon are withered—The vine of Sibmah, the owners of nations, have broken off ruddy branches, Unto Jazer, had they reached, They had spread abroad to the desert,—Her boughs, had stretched forth, had gone over to the sea.

text.NRSV: Therefore I weep with the weeping of Jazer for the vines of Sibmah; I drench you with my tears, O Heshbon and Elealeh; for the shout over your fruit harvest and your grain harvest has ceased.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, will I bewail, in the wailing of Jazer, The vine of Sibmah, I will drench thee with my tears, O Heshbon and Elealeh,—For, upon thy fruit-harvest, and upon thy grain-harvest, the battle-shout, hath fallen.

text.NRSV: Joy and gladness are taken away from the fruitful field; and in the vineyards no songs are sung, no shouts are raised; no treader treads out wine in the presses; I have made the shout-of-joy to cease.
text.Rotherham: Now shall be withdrawn rejoicing and exultation out of the garden-land, And, in the vineyards, shall be neither singing nor shouting,—Wine in the winepress, the treader, shall not tread, The vintage-shout, have I made to cease.

text.NRSV: Therefore my heart throbs like a harp for Moab, and my very self for Kir-heres.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, mine inward parts—for Moab, like a lyre, shall make a plaintive sound,—Yea what is within me, for Kir-heres.

text.NRSV: When Moab presents himself, when he wearies himself upon the high place, when he comes to his sanctuary to pray, he will not prevail.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—When it is seen that Moab hath laboured in vain on the high place, He shall enter into his holy place to pray, and shall not prevail,

text.NRSV: This was the word that the Lord spoke concerning Moab in the past.
text.Rotherham: This, is the word which Yahweh spake concerning Moab in, time past;

text.NRSV: But now the Lord says, In three years, like the years of a hired worker, the glory of Moab will be brought into contempt, in spite of all its great multitude; and those who survive will be very few and feeble.
text.Rotherham: But now, hath Yahweh spoken saying, In three years,—as the years of a hireling, shall the glory of Moab be diminished, with all the great multitude,—even a very small remnant, of no account.

text.NRSV: An oracle concerning Damascus. See, Damascus will cease to be a city, and will become a heap of ruins.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Damascus,—Lo! Damascus, is to be removed from being a city, And shall become a heap of ruins:

text.NRSV: Her towns will be deserted forever; they will be places for flocks, which will lie down, and no one will make them afraid.
text.Rotherham: Forsaken, are the desolate cities,—For flocks, shall they serve, Which shall lie down and have none to make them afraid.

text.NRSV: The fortress will disappear from Ephraim, and the kingdom from Damascus; and the remnant of Aram will be like the glory of the children of Israel, says the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the fortress cease from Ephraim, And the kingdom from Damascus and the remnant of Syria,—Like the glory of the sons of Israel, shall they be,—Declareth Yahweh of hosts.

text.NRSV: On that day the glory of Jacob will be brought low, and the fat of his flesh will grow lean.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, That the glory of Jacob, shall be diminished, And, the fatness of his flesh, shall be wasted;

text.NRSV: And it shall be as when the reaper gathers standing grain and his arm harvests the ears, and as when one gleans the ears of grain in the valley of Rephaim.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass—That, as the harvestman gathereth standing corn, And with his arm—the ears, he reapeth, Yea it shall come to pass—That, so, shall he be who gleaneth ears, in the vale of Rephaim;

text.NRSV: Gleanings will be left in it, as when an olive tree is beaten— two or three berries in the top of the highest bough, four or five on the branches of a fruit tree, says the Lord God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Yet shall there be left therein, a gleaning. As in the beating of an olive-tree,—Two-three berries in the head of the tree-top,—Four—five, among her fruitful boughs, Declareth Yahweh God of Israel.

text.NRSV: On that day people will regard their Maker, and their eyes will look to the Holy One of Israel;
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall the son of earth look to him that made him,—And his eyes unto the Holy One of Israel, be turned;

text.NRSV: they will not regard the altars, the work of their hands, and they will not look to what their own fingers have made, either the sacred poles or the incense altars.
text.Rotherham: And he shall not look unto the altars the work of his own hands,—Nor to what his own fingers have made, shall his eye be turned, Whether Sacred Stems or Sun-pillars.

text.NRSV: On that day their strong cities will be like the deserted places of the Hivites and the Amorites, which they deserted because of the children of Israel, and there will be desolation.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall his fortified cities become Like a neglected bough and a topmost branch, Which they neglected because of the sons of Israel,—So shall there be desolation.

text.NRSV: For you have forgotten the God of your salvation, and have not remembered the Rock of your refuge; therefore, though you plant pleasant plants and set out slips of an alien god,
text.Rotherham: Because thou didst forget the God of thy salvation, And thy Rock of refuge, thou didst not remember, For this cause, shalt thou plant very pleasant plants, And, the slip of a stranger, shalt thou set:

text.NRSV: though you make them grow on the day that you plant them, and make them blossom in the morning that you sow; yet the harvest will flee away in a day of grief and incurable pain.
text.Rotherham: In the day when thou plantest, fence thou in, And in the morning, cause thou, thy slip, to blossom,—A harvest will have waved in the day of destiny, and mortal pain.

text.NRSV: Ah, the thunder of many peoples, they thunder like the thundering of the sea! Ah, the roar of nations, they roar like the roaring of mighty waters!
text.Rotherham: Alas! the booming of many peoples, Like the booming of the seas, shall they boom,—And the rushing of nations! Like the rushing of mighty waters, shall they rush.

text.NRSV: The nations roar like the roaring of many waters, but he will rebuke them, and they will flee far away, chased like chaff on the mountains before the wind and whirling dust before the storm.
text.Rotherham: Though nations like the rushing of many waters, shall rush, Yet shall one rebuke him, And he shall flee far away,—And be chased As the chaff of the mountains before a wind, And as whirling stubble before a storm!

text.NRSV: At evening time, lo, terror! Before morning, they are no more. This is the fate of those who despoil us, and the lot of those who plunder us.
text.Rotherham: At eventide, lo! terror, Before morning, he is not! This, is the portion of them who plunder us, And the lot of them who make of us a prey.

text.NRSV: Ah, land of whirring wings beyond the rivers of Ethiopia,
text.Rotherham: Ho! thou land of the buzzing of wings,—Which is beyond the rivers of Ethiopia:

text.NRSV: sending ambassadors by the Nile in vessels of papyrus on the waters! Go, you swift messengers, to a nation tall and smooth, to a people feared near and far, a nation mighty and conquering, whose land the rivers divide.
text.Rotherham: That sendeth by the sea, ambassadors Even in vessels of paper-reed, upon the face of the waters,—Go ye swift messengers Unto a nation drawn out and polished, Unto a people terrible from their beginning and onwards,—A nation most mighty and subduing, Whose land rivers have cut through.

text.NRSV: All you inhabitants of the world, you who live on the earth, when a signal is raised on the mountains, look! When a trumpet is blown, listen!
text.Rotherham: All ye inhabitants of the world And ye, that dwell in the earth,—When there is lifted up an ensign on the mountains, Ye shall look, And when there is blown a horn, Ye shall hearken.

text.NRSV: For thus the Lord said to me: I will quietly look from my dwelling like clear heat in sunshine, like a cloud of dew in the heat of harvest.
text.Rotherham: For, thus, said Yahweh unto me,—I must be quiet I must look on in my fixed place of abode,—Like a bright heat on the light, Like a cloud of dew in the heat of harvest.

text.NRSV: For before the harvest, when the blossom is over and the flower becomes a ripening grape, he will cut off the shoots with pruning hooks, and the spreading branches he will hew away.
text.Rotherham: For before harvest, when the bud is perfect, And, the blossom, becometh a ripening grape, Then will one cut down the twigs with pruning-hooks, And the tendrils, will he remove [and] cast down:

text.NRSV: They shall all be left to the birds of prey of the mountains and to the wild animals of the earth. And the birds of prey will summer on them, and all the wild animals of the earth will winter on them.
text.Rotherham: They shall be left together. To the ravenous birds, of the mountains, And to the beast of the earth,—Then shall the ravenous bird, summer upon them, And, every beast of the earth, upon them, shall winter.

text.NRSV: At that time gifts will be brought to the Lord of hosts from a people tall and smooth, from a people feared near and far, a nation mighty and conquering, whose land the rivers divide, to Mount Zion, the place of the name of the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: In that time, shall there be borne along. As a present unto Yahweh of hosts A people drawn out and polished, Even from a people terrible from their beginning and onwards,—nation most mighty and subduing Whose land rivers, have cut through, Unto the place of the Name of Yahweh of hosts. Mount Zion.

text.NRSV: An oracle concerning Egypt. See, the Lord is riding on a swift cloud and comes to Egypt; the idols of Egypt will tremble at his presence, and the heart of the Egyptians will melt within them.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Egypt: Lo! Yahweh, riding upon a swift cloud, and he will enter Egypt, And the idols of Egypt shall shake at his presence, And, the heart of Egypt, shall melt within him;

text.NRSV: I will stir up Egyptians against Egyptians, and they will fight, one against the other, neighbor against neighbor, city against city, kingdom against kingdom;
text.Rotherham: And I will stir up Egyptians against Egyptians, And they shall fight—Every one against his brother and Every one against his neighbour,—City against city, and Kingdom against kingdom.

text.NRSV: the spirit of the Egyptians within them will be emptied out, and I will confound their plans; and they will consult the idols and the spirits of the dead and the ghosts and the familiar spirits;
text.Rotherham: And the spirit of Egypt, shall vanish, within them, Yea the sagacity thereof, will I swallow up,—And they will seek Unto the idols and Unto them that mutter, and Unto them that have familiar spirits, and Unto the wizards;

text.NRSV: I will deliver the Egyptians into the hand of a hard master; a fierce king will rule over them, says the Lord, the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: And I will deliver the Egyptians into the hand of a cruel lord,—And a fierce king shall rule over them, Declareth the Lord, Yahweh of hosts.

text.NRSV: The waters of the Nile will be dried up, and the river will be parched and dry;
text.Rotherham: And the waters shall be dried up from the great stream,—And the River, shall waste and be dry;

text.NRSV: its canals will become foul, and the branches of Egypt’s Nile will diminish and dry up, reeds and rushes will rot away.
text.Rotherham: And rivers, shall stink, The canals of Egypt be shallow and waste, Reed and rush, be withered;

text.NRSV: There will be bare places by the Nile, on the brink of the Nile; and all that is sown by the Nile will dry up, be blown away, and be no more.
text.Rotherham: The meadows by the Nile, by the mouth of the Nile. And all that is sown in the Nile, Shall be dry, driven away, and not be!

text.NRSV: The fishers will mourn and lament, all who cast hooks in the Nile; and those who spread nets on the water will languish.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the fishers, lament, And all shall mourn who cast in the Nile a hook,—And they who spread nets on the face of the waters shall languish;

text.NRSV: The workers in flax will be in despair, and the carders and those at the loom will grow pale.
text.Rotherham: Then shall turn pale The workers in combed flax,—and The weavers of white linen;

text.NRSV: Its pillars will be crushed, and all who work for hire will be grieved.
text.Rotherham: Then shall her pillars be crushed,—All who make wages, be bowed down in soul.

text.NRSV: The princes of Zoan are utter fools; the wise counselors of Pharaoh give stupid advice. How can you say to Pharaoh, “I am one of the sages, a descendant of ancient kings”?
text.Rotherham: Surely, foolish, are the princes of Zoan, the wisest counsellors of Pharaoh, in counsel are brutish,—How can ye say unto Pharaoh, Son of the wise, am I Son of the kings of olden time?

text.NRSV: Where now are your sages? Let them tell you and make known what the Lord of hosts has planned against Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Where then are thy wise men? Pray let them tell thee! And let them know what Yahweh of hosts hath purposed on Egypt!

text.NRSV: The princes of Zoan have become fools, and the princes of Memphis are deluded; those who are the cornerstones of her tribes have led Egypt astray.
text.Rotherham: Doting are the princes of Zoan, Deceived are the princes of Noph: They who are the corner-stone of her tribes, have led Egypt astray.

text.NRSV: The Lord has poured into her a spirit of confusion; and they have made Egypt stagger in all its doings as a drunkard staggers around in vomit.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, hath infused in her midst, a spirit of perverseness,—And they have led Egypt astray into all his own doings, As a drunken man staggereth into his own vomit;

text.NRSV: Neither head nor tail, palm branch or reed, will be able to do anything for Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Egypt shall have nothing which can be done, Which head or tail, palm-top or rush, can do!

text.NRSV: On that day the Egyptians will be like women, and tremble with fear before the hand that the Lord of hosts shakes over them.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall Egypt be like unto women,—And shall start and tremble because of the brandishing of the hand of Yahweh of hosts, which he is about to brandish over it.

text.NRSV: And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians; everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the plan that the Lord of hosts is planning against them.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the soil of Judah become, to Egypt, a terror; Every one to whom it is mentioned, will tremble,—Because of the purpose of Yahweh of hosts, which he is purposing against it.

text.NRSV: On that day there will be five cities in the land of Egypt that speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the Lord of hosts. One of these will be called the City of the Sun.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall there be five cities in the land of Egypt Speaking the language of Canaan, And swearing unto Yahweh of hosts,—The city of destruction, shall be the name of one!

text.NRSV: On that day there will be an altar to the Lord in the center of the land of Egypt, and a pillar to the Lord at its border.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall there be An altar unto Yahweh, in the midst of the land of Egypt,—And a pillar near the boundary thereof unto Yahweh;

text.NRSV: It will be a sign and a witness to the Lord of hosts in the land of Egypt; when they cry to the Lord because of oppressors, he will send them a savior, and will defend and deliver them.
text.Rotherham: And it shall become a sign and a witness unto Yahweh of hosts in the land of Egypt,—For they will make outcry unto Yahweh, because of oppressors, That he would send them a saviour—and a great one Then will he deliver them.

text.NRSV: The Lord will make himself known to the Egyptians; and the Egyptians will know the Lord on that day, and will worship with sacrifice and burnt offering, and they will make vows to the Lord and perform them.
text.Rotherham: Then will, Yahweh, make himself known, to the Egyptians, So shall the Egyptians know, Yahweh, in that day,—And they will offer a sacrifice and a present And will vow a vow unto Yahweh and will perform.

text.NRSV: The Lord will strike Egypt, striking and healing; they will return to the Lord, and he will listen to their supplications and heal them.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh, will plague Egypt, plague and heal,—And they will turn unto Yahweh And he will be entreated of them and will heal them.

text.NRSV: On that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria, and the Assyrian will come into Egypt, and the Egyptian into Assyria, and the Egyptians will worship with the Assyrians.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall there be a highway. from Egypt to Assyria, And, the Assyrians, shall come into Egypt, And, the Egyptians, into Assyria; And, the Egyptians shall serve, with the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: On that day Israel will be the third with Egypt and Assyria, a blessing in the midst of the earth,
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall, Israel, be, a third, with Egypt and with Assyria,—A blessing in the midst of the earth:

text.NRSV: whom the Lord of hosts has blessed, saying, “Blessed be Egypt my people, and Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel my heritage.”
text.Rotherham: Whom Yahweh of hosts hath blessed saying,—Blessed, be My people—the Egyptians, And the work of my hands—the Assyrians, And mine own inheritance—Israel.

text.NRSV: In the year that the commander-in-chief, who was sent by King Sargon of Assyria, came to Ashdod and fought against it and took it—
text.Rotherham: In the year that Tartan entered Ashdod, when Sargon king of Assyria, sent him,—and he fought against Ashdod and captured it,—

text.NRSV: at that time the Lord had spoken to Isaiah son of Amoz, saying, “Go, and loose the sackcloth from your loins and take your sandals off your feet,” and he had done so, walking naked and barefoot.
text.Rotherham: At that time, spake Yahweh, through Isaiah son of Amoz, saying, Go, and loose the sackcloth from off thy loins, And thy sandal, draw thou off from thy foot,—And he did so, walking disrobed and barefoot.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “Just as my servant Isaiah has walked naked and barefoot for three years as a sign and a portent against Egypt and Ethiopia,
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh,—As my servant Isaiah, hath walked, disrobed and barefoot three years as a sign and a wonder against Egypt and against Ethiopia,

text.NRSV: so shall the king of Assyria lead away the Egyptians as captives and the Ethiopians as exiles, both the young and the old, naked and barefoot, with buttocks uncovered, to the shame of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: So, shall the king of Assyria lead away the captives of Egypt and the exiles of Ethiopia young and old disrobed and barefoot, with their persons behind Uncovered the shame of Egypt.

text.NRSV: Then they shall be dismayed and confounded because of Ethiopia their hope and Egypt their boast.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall they be confounded and turn pale,—For Ethiopia, their expectation, and For Egypt their boast;

text.NRSV: On that day the inhabitants of this coastland will say, ‘See, this is what has happened to those in whom we hoped and to whom we fled for help and deliverance from the king of Assyria! And we, how shall we escape?’ ”
text.Rotherham: And the inhabitant of this shore, shall exclaim, in that day, Lo! such, is our expectation, whereunto we fled for help, that we might be delivered from the presence of the king of Assyria! How then shall, we, escape?

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning the desert of the sea. As whirlwinds in the Negeb sweep on, it comes from the desert, from a terrible land.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on the desert of the sea: As storm-winds in the South which with a rush from the desert, do come from a terrible land,

text.NRSV: A stern vision is declared to me; the betrayer betrays, and the destroyer destroys. Go up, O Elam, lay siege, O Media; all the sighing she has caused I bring to an end.
text.Rotherham: [So] hath, a grievous vision, been told me:—the deceiver, is deceiving. And the spoiler, is spoiling, Go up, O Elam Besiege O Media, All the sighing she hath caused, have I made to cease.

text.NRSV: Therefore my loins are filled with anguish; pangs have seized me, like the pangs of a woman in labor; I am bowed down so that I cannot hear, I am dismayed so that I cannot see.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, are my loins filled with anguish, Pangs, have seized me, as the pangs of her that is giving birth,—I writhe so that I cannot hear, I tremble, so that I cannot see:

text.NRSV: My mind reels, horror has appalled me; the twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling.
text.Rotherham: My heart fluttereth, A horror, terrifieth me,—My twilight of pleasure, hath he turned for me into a time of trembling.

text.NRSV: They prepare the table, they spread the rugs, they eat, they drink. Rise up, commanders, oil the shield!
text.Rotherham: [Ye thought] to prepare the table—spread the mat—eat—drink! …Arise ye chieftains anoint the shield!

text.NRSV: For thus the Lord said to me: “Go, set a watchman, let him announce what he sees.
text.Rotherham: For, thus, hath My Lord said unto me,—Go, set the watchman, What he seeth, let him tell!

text.NRSV: When he sees riders, horsemen in pairs, riders on donkeys, riders on camels, let him listen diligently, very diligently.”
text.Rotherham: When he seeth A train of horsemen in double rank, A train of asses, A train of camels, Then shall he hearken attentively with diligent heed.

text.NRSV: Then the watcher called out: “Upon a watchtower, O Lord, I stand continually by day, and at my post I am stationed throughout the night.
text.Rotherham: Then cried he. A lion! On the watch, O My Lord, had I been standing continually, by day, And at my post, had I been stationed whole nights;—

text.NRSV: See, there they come, riders, horsemen in pairs!” Then he responded, “Fallen, fallen is Babylon; and all the images of her gods lie shattered on the ground.”
text.Rotherham: When lo! here was a train of men coming. With horsemen in double rank,—And one began and said, Fallen! fallen! is Babylon, And all the images of her gods, are smashed to the ground!

text.NRSV: O my threshed and winnowed one, what I have heard from the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, I announce to you.
text.Rotherham: O thou My threshing! And the grain of my corn-floor! That which I have heard from Yahweh of hosts, the God of Israel, Have I declared, unto you.

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning Dumah. One is calling to me from Seir, “Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?”
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Dumah: Unto me, is one crying—out of Seir, Watchman! how far gone is the night? Watchman, how far gone is the night?

text.NRSV: The watchman says: “Morning comes, and also the night. If you will inquire, inquire; come back again.”
text.Rotherham: Said the watchman, There cometh a morning. But also a night,—If ye will enquire, enquire, ye—Come, again!

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning the desert plain. In the scrub of the desert plain you will lodge, O caravans of Dedanites.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Arabia,—Among the shrubs in Arabia, must ye lodge, Ye caravans of Dedanites.

text.NRSV: Bring water to the thirsty, meet the fugitive with bread, O inhabitants of the land of Tema.
text.Rotherham: To meet the thirsty, bring ye water,—Ye dwellers in the land of Tema; With bread for him, get in advance of him that is in flight!

text.NRSV: For they have fled from the swords, from the drawn sword, from the bent bow, and from the stress of battle.
text.Rotherham: For, before swords, have they fled: Before a sword that is drawn, Before a bow that is bent, And before the stress of war.

text.NRSV: For thus the Lord said to me: Within a year, according to the years of a hired worker, all the glory of Kedar will come to an end;
text.Rotherham: For, thus, hath My Lord said unto me,—Within a year according to the yearn of a hireling, shall fail all the glory of Kedar;

text.NRSV: and the remaining bows of Kedar’s warriors will be few; for the Lord, the God of Israel, has spoken.
text.Rotherham: And, the remnant of the record of bowmen, The heroes of the sons of Kedar, shall become few; For Yahweh God of Israel, hath spoken.

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning the valley of vision. What do you mean that you have gone up, all of you, to the housetops,
text.Rotherham: The oracle on the valley of vision,—What aileth thee, then, That thou art wholly gone up to the house-tops?

text.NRSV: you that are full of shoutings, tumultuous city, exultant town? Your slain are not slain by the sword, nor are they dead in battle.
text.Rotherham: With tumults, art thou filled, thou citadel in commotion! city exultant! Thy slain, are Not the slain, of the sword, Nor the dead in battle.

text.NRSV: Your leaders have all fled together; they were captured without the use of a bow. All of you who were found were captured together, though they had fled far away.
text.Rotherham: All thy ruler, having fled, together, by the bow, are taken captive: All found in thee have been taken captive, together, Far away, have they fled.

text.NRSV: Therefore I said: “Look away from me, let me weep bitter tears; do not try to comfort me for the destruction of my people.”
text.Rotherham: For this cause, I said—Look away from me, Bitterly, will I weep,—Do not press to comfort me, For the ruin of the daughter of my people.

text.NRSV: For the Lord God of hosts has a day of tumult and trampling and confusion in the valley of vision, a battering down of walls and a cry for help to the mountains.
text.Rotherham: For a day of confusion and downtreading and perplexity, pertaineth to My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, in the valley of vision, an undermining of walls, and a crying for help to the mountain.

text.NRSV: Elam bore the quiver with chariots and horsemen, and Kir uncovered the shield.
text.Rotherham: Yea, Elam, beareth the quiver, With trains of men, [and] horsemen,—And, Kir, hath uncovered the shield.

text.NRSV: Your choicest valleys were full of chariots, and the horsemen took their stand at the gates.
text.Rotherham: And it hath come to pass that the choice of thy vales, are full of chariots; Yea, the horsemen, have set themselves, in array, at the gate.

text.NRSV: He has taken away the covering of Judah. On that day you looked to the weapons of the House of the Forest,
text.Rotherham: Then removed he the veil of Judah,—Yea thou didst peer on that day, into the armoury of the forest-house;

text.NRSV: and you saw that there were many breaches in the city of David, and you collected the waters of the lower pool.
text.Rotherham: And the breaches in the city of David, ye beheld for they were many,—So ye gathered together the waters of the lower pool;

text.NRSV: You counted the houses of Jerusalem, and you broke down the houses to fortify the wall.
text.Rotherham: And the houses of Jerusalem, ye counted,—And brake down the houses, to fortify the wall;

text.NRSV: You made a reservoir between the two walls for the water of the old pool. But you did not look to him who did it, or have regard for him who planned it long ago.
text.Rotherham: And a reservoir, ye made between the two walls, for the waters of the ancient pool,—And had no regard unto him that made it, Nor unto him that formed it long ago, had ye respect.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord God of hosts called for weeping and mourning, for baldness and putting on sackcloth;
text.Rotherham: And, when My Lord Yahweh of hosts called in that day,—for weeping, and for lamentation, and for shaving bare and for girding with sackcloth,

text.NRSV: but instead there was joy and festivity, killing oxen and slaughtering sheep, eating meat and drinking wine. “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
text.Rotherham: Then lo! joy and rejoicing, killing oxen and slaughtering sheep, eating flesh and drinking wine,—Let us eat and drink, For to-morrow, we may die!

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts has revealed himself in my ears: “Surely this iniquity will not be forgiven you until you die,” says the Lord God of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Therefore did Yahweh reveal himself in mine ears. Surely there shall be no propitiatory-covering put over this iniquity for you until ye die, Saith My Lord Yahweh of hosts.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord God of hosts: Come, go to this steward, to Shebna, who is master of the household, and say to him:
text.Rotherham: Thus, said My Lord Yahweh, of hosts, Come go in unto this steward, Unto Shebna, who is over the house:

text.NRSV: What have you to do here, and whom have you here, that you have cut out a tomb here for yourself, cutting a tomb on the height, and carving a habitation for yourself in the rock?
text.Rotherham: What doest thou here? And whom hast thou here? That thou hast hewn for thyself here a sepulchre,—As one hewing on high his sepulchre, Cutting out in the cliff a habitation for himself?

text.NRSV: The Lord is about to hurl you away violently, my man. He will seize firm hold of you,
text.Rotherham: Lo! Yahweh, is about to hurl thee, with a hurl, O mighty man,—And roll thee with a roll;

text.NRSV: whirl you round and round, and throw you like a ball into a wide land; there you shall die, and there your splendid chariots shall lie, O you disgrace to your master’s house!
text.Rotherham: He will, toss, thee, with a toss, like a ball, into a country wide on both hands,—There, shalt thou die, And there shall thy glorious chariots be the contempt of the house of thy lord.

text.NRSV: I will thrust you from your office, and you will be pulled down from your post.
text.Rotherham: Thus will I thrust thee out from thine office,—And from thy station, shall one tear thee down.

text.NRSV: On that day I will call my servant Eliakim son of Hilkiah,
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, That I will call for my servant, for Eliakim son of Hilkiah,

text.NRSV: and will clothe him with your robe and bind your sash on him. I will commit your authority to his hand, and he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem and to the house of Judah.
text.Rotherham: And will clothe him with thy tunic And with thy girdle, will I gird him, And thine authority, will I deliver into his hand,—So shall he become a father To the inhabitant of Jerusalem and To the house of Judah.

text.NRSV: I will place on his shoulder the key of the house of David; he shall open, and no one shall shut; he shall shut, and no one shall open.
text.Rotherham: And I will lay the key of the house of David upon his shoulder,—And he shall open and none shall shut, And shut and none shall open;

text.NRSV: I will fasten him like a peg in a secure place, and he will become a throne of honor to his ancestral house.
text.Rotherham: And I will fasten him as a peg in a sure place,—And he shall become a throne of glory, to the house of his father;

text.NRSV: And they will hang on him the whole weight of his ancestral house, the offspring and issue, every small vessel, from the cups to all the flagons.
text.Rotherham: And they shall hang upon him all the weight of his father’s house—The offshoots and the side-twigs—All the small vessels, Both the cups and all the jugs

text.NRSV: On that day, says the Lord of hosts, the peg that was fastened in a secure place will give way; it will be cut down and fall, and the load that was on it will perish, for the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: In that day,—Declareth Yahweh of hosts Shall the peg, give way, that was fastened in a sure place,—Yea it shall be cut off and fall And the burden that was upon it shall perish, For, Yahweh, hath spoken!

text.NRSV: The oracle concerning Tyre. Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for your fortress is destroyed. When they came from Cyprus, they learned of it.
text.Rotherham: The oracle on Tyre,—Howl! ye ships of Tarshish, For it is laid too waste to be a haven to enter, From the land of Cyprus, hath it been unveiled to them.

text.NRSV: Be still, O inhabitants of the coast, O merchants of Sidon, your messengers crossed over the sea
text.Rotherham: Be dumb, ye inhabitants of the Coast,—Whom the merchants of Zidon, passing over the sea, once replenished;

text.NRSV: and were on the mighty waters; your revenue was the grain of Shihor, the harvest of the Nile; you were the merchant of the nations.
text.Rotherham: Yea on mighty waters, was the grain of Shihor, The harvest of the Nile, was her increase,—And so she became a mart of nations.

text.NRSV: Be ashamed, O Sidon, for the sea has spoken, the fortress of the sea, saying: “I have neither labored nor given birth, I have neither reared young men nor brought up young women.”
text.Rotherham: Turn thou pale, O Zidon, For spoken hath the sea, the fortress of the sea saying,—I have neither been in pangs nor given birth I have neither brought up young men nor promoted virgins.

text.NRSV: When the report comes to Egypt, they will be in anguish over the report about Tyre.
text.Rotherham: Like the report of Egypt, They shall be in pangs at the like report of Tyre.

text.NRSV: Cross over to Tarshish; wail, O inhabitants of the coast!
text.Rotherham: Pass ye over to Tarshish,—Howl ye inhabitants of the Coast:

text.NRSV: Is this your exultant city whose origin is from days of old, whose feet carried her to settle far away?
text.Rotherham: Is this to you an exultation? Though from ancient days, is her antiquity, Yet shall her own feet carry her away, far off to dwell.

text.NRSV: Who has planned this against Tyre, the bestower of crowns, whose merchants were princes, whose traders were the honored of the earth?
text.Rotherham: Who hath purposed this, against Tyre, The bestower of crowns,—Whose merchants are princes, Her traders the honourable of the earth?

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts has planned it— to defile the pride of all glory, to dishonor all the honored of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh of hosts, hath purposed it,—To humble the pride of all beauty, To make of little esteem all the honourable of the earth.

text.NRSV: Cross over to your own land, O ships of Tarshish; there is no more restraint.
text.Rotherham: Pass through thy land as the Nile,—O daughter of Tarshish, there is no restraint any longer!

text.NRSV: He has stretched out his hand over the sea, he has shaken the kingdoms; the Lord has given command concerning Canaan to destroy its fortresses.
text.Rotherham: His hand, hath he stretched out over the sea, He hath shaken kingdoms,—Yahweh, hath given command against the Phoenician coast, To destroy her fortresses.

text.NRSV: He said: “You will exult no more, O oppressed virgin daughter Sidon; rise, cross over to Cyprus— even there you will have no rest.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore hath he said,—No more, again, do thou exult, Thou violated virgin daughter of Zidon,—To Cyprus, arise and pass over, Even there, shall one find thee no rest.

text.NRSV: Look at the land of the Chaldeans! This is the people; it was not Assyria. They destined Tyre for wild animals. They erected their siege-towers, they stripped her palaces bare, they made her a ruin.
text.Rotherham: Lo! the land of the Chaldeans, This is the people that was not, Assyria, founded it for the inhabitants of the desert,—They set up its siege-towers, They demolished its palaces Made it a ruin!

text.NRSV: Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for your fortress is destroyed.
text.Rotherham: Howl, ye ships of Tarshish,—For laid waste is your fortress.

text.NRSV: From that day Tyre will be forgotten for seventy years, like the lifetime of one king. At the end of seventy years, it will happen to Tyre as in the song of the prostitute:
text.Rotherham: So shall it be in that day, That Tyre shall be forgotten seventy years, According to the days of a certain king: At the end of seventy years, shall it befall Tyre according to the song of the harlot:

text.NRSV: Take a harp, go about the city, you forgotten prostitute! Make sweet melody, sing many songs, that you may be remembered.
text.Rotherham: Take thou a lyre, Go round the city, O harlot forgotten,—Sweetly touch the strings Lengthen out the song, That thou mayest be called to mind.

text.NRSV: At the end of seventy years, the Lord will visit Tyre, and she will return to her hire, and will prostitute herself with all the kingdoms of the world on the face of the earth.
text.Rotherham: So shall it be, at the end of seventy years, That Yahweh will visit Tyre, And she will return to her hire,—Yea she will play the harlot—with all the kingdoms of the earth, upon the face of the ground.

text.NRSV: Her merchandise and her hire will be dedicated to the Lord; it will not be stored or hoarded, but her merchandise will supply abundant food and fine clothing for those who live in the presence of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But her merchandise and her hire, shall be hallowed unto Yahweh, It shall not be stored up, nor hoarded,—For, to them who dwell before Yahweh, shall her merchandise belong, That they may eat to satisfaction And have stately apparel.

text.NRSV: See, the Lord is about to lay waste the earth and make it desolate, and he will twist its surface and scatter its inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: Lo! Yahweh emptying the earth and laying it waste,—And he will overturn the face thereof, And scatter them who dwell therein.

text.NRSV: And it shall be, as with the people, so with the priest; as with the slave, so with the master; as with the maid, so with her mistress; as with the buyer, so with the seller; as with the lender, so with the borrower; as with the creditor, so with the debtor.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be—As the people, so, the priest, As the servant, so his lord, As the maid, so, her mistress,—As the buyer, so, the seller, As the lender, so, the borrower, As the debtor, so! his creditor.

text.NRSV: The earth shall be utterly laid waste and utterly despoiled; for the Lord has spoken this word.
text.Rotherham: Emptied—emptied—shall be the earth yea pillaged—pillaged,—For, Yahweh, hath spoken this word.

text.NRSV: The earth dries up and withers, the world languishes and withers; the heavens languish together with the earth.
text.Rotherham: Mourneth, fadeth, the earth Languisheth, fadeth, the world,—Languished have the lofty of the people of the earth.

text.NRSV: The earth lies polluted under its inhabitants; for they have transgressed laws, violated the statutes, broken the everlasting covenant.
text.Rotherham: Yea the earth itself is profaned under them who dwell therein,—For they have Set aside laws, Gone beyond statute, Broken an age-abiding covenant.

text.NRSV: Therefore a curse devours the earth, and its inhabitants suffer for their guilt; therefore the inhabitants of the earth dwindle, and few people are left.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, a curse, hath devoured the earth, And punished are the dwellers therein,—For this cause, are burned the inhabitants of the earth, And the men left remaining—are few.

text.NRSV: The wine dries up, the vine languishes, all the merry-hearted sigh.
text.Rotherham: Mourneth the new wine. Withereth the vine,—Sighing are all the merryhearted:

text.NRSV: The mirth of the tambourines is stilled, the noise of the jubilant has ceased, the mirth of the lyre is stilled.
text.Rotherham: Ceased hath the mirth of timbrels, Ended is the noise of the uproarious,—Ceased hath the mirth of the lyre:

text.NRSV: No longer do they drink wine with singing; strong drink is bitter to those who drink it.
text.Rotherham: With a song, they drink not wine,—Bitter is strong drink, to them who drink it:

text.NRSV: The city of chaos is broken down, every house is shut up so that no one can enter.
text.Rotherham: Broken down is the city of desolation,—Shut up every house that it cannot be entered.

text.NRSV: There is an outcry in the streets for lack of wine; all joy has reached its eventide; the gladness of the earth is banished.
text.Rotherham: There is an outcry concerning wine in the streets,—Darkened is all joy, Departed the gladness of the earth.

text.NRSV: Desolation is left in the city, the gates are battered into ruins.
text.Rotherham: There is left in the city. desolation,—And to ruins, have been broken the gate.

text.NRSV: For thus it shall be on the earth and among the nations, as when an olive tree is beaten, as at the gleaning when the grape harvest is ended.
text.Rotherham: When, thus, it shall be in the earth in the midst of the peoples, [There shall be] like the shaking of an olive-tree, like the going round to pick when closed is the harvest.

text.NRSV: They lift up their voices, they sing for joy; they shout from the west over the majesty of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: They, shall lift up their voice—shall raise a tremulous note,—On account of the splendour of Yahweh, have they made a shrill cry, on the West;

text.NRSV: Therefore in the east give glory to the Lord; in the coastlands of the sea, to the name of the Lord, the God of Israel.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, In the Regions of Light, give ye glory to Yahweh,—In the Coastlands of the Sea, unto the Name of Yahweh. God of Israel,

text.NRSV: From the ends of the earth we hear songs of praise, of glory to the Righteous One. But I say, I pine away, I pine away. Woe is me! For the treacherous betray, with much treachery the treacherous betray.
text.Rotherham: From the uttermost part of the earth, melodies, have we heard—Beauty, to the righteous one! But I had said—Ruin to me! Ruin to me! Woe to me! Traitors, have betrayed, Yea traitorously, have traitors betrayed!

text.NRSV: Terror, and the pit, and the snare are upon you, O inhabitant of the earth!
text.Rotherham: Terror and pit and snare,—are upon thee, O inhabitant of the earth!

text.NRSV: Whoever flees at the sound of the terror shall fall into the pit; and whoever climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For the windows of heaven are opened, and the foundations of the earth tremble.
text.Rotherham: So shall it be—He that fleeth from the sound of the terror! shall fall into the pit, And, he that getteth up out of the midst of the pit, shall be captured in the snare,—For, the windows on high, have opened, And shaken are the foundations of earth.

text.NRSV: The earth is utterly broken, the earth is torn asunder, the earth is violently shaken.
text.Rotherham: The earth breaketh, breaketh,—The earth crasheth, crasheth, The earth tottereth, tottereth;

text.NRSV: The earth staggers like a drunkard, it sways like a hut; its transgression lies heavy upon it, and it falls, and will not rise again.
text.Rotherham: The earth staggereth—staggereth like a drunken man, And rocketh to and fro like a night-hut,—So shall be heavy upon her, her transgression, And she shall fall and not again rise.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord will punish the host of heaven in heaven, and on earth the kings of the earth.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be in that day, That Yahweh will bring punishment Upon the host of the height in the height,—And upon the kings of the ground on the ground.

text.NRSV: They will be gathered together as prisoners in a pit; they will be shut up in a prison, and after many days they will be punished.
text.Rotherham: And they shall be swept together in a crowd, fettered for a pit, And shall be lowered into a dungeon,—And, after many days, shall they be punished.

text.NRSV: Then the moon will be abashed, and the sun ashamed; for the Lord of hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and before his elders he will manifest his glory.
text.Rotherham: Then shall blush the silvery moon, Then turn pale the glowing sun,—Because Yahweh of hosts hath become king In Mount Zion And in Jerusalem, And before his Elders in glory,

text.NRSV: O Lord, you are my God; I will exalt you, I will praise your name; for you have done wonderful things, plans formed of old, faithful and sure.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh! my God, thou art, I will exalt thee, I will praise thy Name, For thou hast done a wonderful thing,—Purposes of long ago Faithfulness in truth.

text.NRSV: For you have made the city a heap, the fortified city a ruin; the palace of aliens is a city no more, it will never be rebuilt.
text.Rotherham: For thou hast made, of a citadel, a mound, of a defenced city, a ruin,—palaces for foreigners to be no city, To times age-abiding, shall it not be built.

text.NRSV: Therefore strong peoples will glorify you; cities of ruthless nations will fear you.
text.Rotherham: For this cause! shall glorify thee—a strong people, the city of tyrannous nations shall revere thee;

text.NRSV: For you have been a refuge to the poor, a refuge to the needy in their distress, a shelter from the rainstorm and a shade from the heat. When the blast of the ruthless was like a winter rainstorm,
text.Rotherham: For thou didst become A refuge to the weak. A refuge to the needy, when distress was upon him,—A shelter from the storm. A shade from the heat, When the blast of tyrants was like a storm against a wall.

text.NRSV: the noise of aliens like heat in a dry place, you subdued the heat with the shade of a cloud; the song of the ruthless was stilled.
text.Rotherham: As heat in a desert, the pomp of foreigners, wilt thou subdue,—Heat—with the shade of a cloud, The song of tyrants! become low.

text.NRSV: On this mountain the Lord of hosts will make for all peoples a feast of rich food, a feast of well-aged wines, of rich food filled with marrow, of well-aged wines strained clear.
text.Rotherham: Then will Yahweh of hosts prepare for all the peoples in this mountain, A banquet of fat things, A banquet of old wines,—Of fat things full of marrow, Of old wines well refined;

text.NRSV: And he will destroy on this mountain the shroud that is cast over all peoples, the sheet that is spread over all nations; he will swallow up death forever.
text.Rotherham: And he will swallow up in this mountain, The mask of the veil, the veil that is upon all the peoples,—And the web that is woven over all the nations.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord God will wipe away the tears from all faces, and the disgrace of his people he will take away from all the earth, for the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: Having swallowed up death itself victoriously, My Lord, Yahweh, will wipe away, tears from off all faces,—And the reproach of his own people, will he remove from off all the earth, For, Yahweh, hath spoken.

text.NRSV: It will be said on that day, Lo, this is our God; we have waited for him, so that he might save us. This is the Lord for whom we have waited; let us be glad and rejoice in his salvation.
text.Rotherham: So shall it be said, in that day, Lo! our God, is this! We waited for him, that he might save us,—This, is Yahweh! We waited for him, Let us exult and rejoice in his salvation.

text.NRSV: For the hand of the Lord will rest on this mountain. The Moabites shall be trodden down in their place as straw is trodden down in a dung-pit.
text.Rotherham: For the hand of Yahweh will settle down in this mountain,—Then shall Moab be trodden down in its place, Like the treading down of a strawheap in the water of a dunghill;

text.NRSV: Even if they spread out their hands in the midst of it, as swimmers spread out their hands to swim, their pride will be laid low despite the struggle of their hands.
text.Rotherham: Should he spread forth his hands in the midst thereof, As a swimmer spreadeth forth to swim, Then would be laid low his pride, together with the devices of his hands.

text.NRSV: The high fortifications of your walls will be brought down, laid low, cast to the ground, even to the dust.
text.Rotherham: Yea, the lofty stronghold of thy walls, Hath he brought down—Laid low—Levelled to the ground even unto the dust.

text.NRSV: On that day this song will be sung in the land of Judah: We have a strong city; he sets up victory like walls and bulwarks.
text.Rotherham: In that day, shall be sung this song, in the land of Judah,—A strong city, have we! Salvation, will he set for walls and rampart,

text.NRSV: Open the gates, so that the righteous nation that keeps faith may enter in.
text.Rotherham: Open ye the gates,—That there may enter in—a righteous nation preserving fidelity.

text.NRSV: Those of steadfast mind you keep in peace—in peace because they trust in you.
text.Rotherham: A purpose sustained, thou wilt guard, [saying], Prosper! Prosper! Because in thee, hath he been led to trust.

text.NRSV: Trust in the Lord forever, for in the Lord God you have an everlasting rock.
text.Rotherham: Trust ye in Yahweh, unto futurity,—For, in Yah, Yahweh, is a rock of ages.

text.NRSV: For he has brought low the inhabitants of the height; the lofty city he lays low. He lays it low to the ground, casts it to the dust.
text.Rotherham: For he hath brought down the inhabitants of the height the city exalted,—He layeth it low, Layeth it low even to the ground, Levelleth it, even to the dust:

text.NRSV: The foot tramples it, the feet of the poor, the steps of the needy.
text.Rotherham: The foot trampleth it,—The feet of the lowly, The steps of the weak,

text.NRSV: The way of the righteous is level; O Just One, you make smooth the path of the righteous.
text.Rotherham: The path of a righteous man, is, even,—O Upright One! the track of a righteous man, thou makest level.

text.NRSV: In the path of your judgments, O Lord, we wait for you; your name and your renown are the soul’s desire.
text.Rotherham: Surely in the path of thy regulations, O Yahweh, we waited for thee,—Unto thy Name and unto thy Memorial, was there a longing of soul:

text.NRSV: My soul yearns for you in the night, my spirit within me earnestly seeks you. For when your judgments are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world learn righteousness.
text.Rotherham: With my soul, longed I for thee in the night, Yea with my spirit within me, I kept on searching for thee,—For, when thy regulations [extend] to the earth, The inhabitants of the world will have learned, righteousness.

text.NRSV: If favor is shown to the wicked, they do not learn righteousness; in the land of uprightness they deal perversely and do not see the majesty of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Let favour be shewed to the lawless, he hath not learned righteousness, In a land of honest dealings, he acteth perversely,—And seeth not the splendour of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: O Lord, your hand is lifted up, but they do not see it. Let them see your zeal for your people and be ashamed. Let the fire for your adversaries consume them.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh though thy hand be lifted up,—yet do they not see, Would they might see—and turn pale at a people’s zeal,—Surely, the fire of thine enemies, must consume them!

text.NRSV: O Lord, you will ordain peace for us, for indeed, all that we have done, you have done for us.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, thou wilt ensure prosperity for us,—For even all our works, hast thou wrought for us,

text.NRSV: O Lord our God, other lords besides you have ruled over us, but we acknowledge your name alone.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh, our God! Lords other than thee, have owned us,—By thyself alone, will we call upon thy Name.

text.NRSV: The dead do not live; shades do not rise—because you have punished and destroyed them, and wiped out all memory of them.
text.Rotherham: The dead, come not to life again, The shades, do not arise,—Therefore, thou hast visited and destroyed them, And caused to perish every memorial of them.

text.NRSV: But you have increased the nation, O Lord, you have increased the nation; you are glorified; you have enlarged all the borders of the land.
text.Rotherham: Thou hast increased the nation, O Yahweh, Thou hast increased the nation thou hast gotten thyself glory, Thou hast extended far, all the ends of the land.

text.NRSV: O Lord, in distress they sought you, they poured out a prayer when your chastening was on them.
text.Rotherham: O Yahweh! in distress, they sought thee,—They poured out a whispered prayer, when thy chastening was upon them.

text.NRSV: As a woman with child draws near her time, writhes and cries out in her pangs, so were we because of you, O Lord;
text.Rotherham: Like as a woman with child—Draweth near to giving birth, Is in pain, Crieth out in her pangs So, were we before thee, O Yahweh;—

text.NRSV: we were with child, we writhed, but we gave birth only to wind. We have won no victories on earth, and no one is born to inhabit the world.
text.Rotherham: We were with child—We were in pain, As it were we brought forth wind,—Salvation, we could not accomplish for the earth, Neither were born the inhabitants of the world.

text.NRSV: Your dead shall live, their corpses shall rise. Utterly awake and sing for joy, you who dwell in the dust! For your dew is a radiant dew, and the earth will give birth to those long dead.
text.Rotherham: Thy dead, shall come to life again, My dead body, they shall arise,—Awake and shout for joy, ye that dwell in the dust For, a dew of light, is thy dew, And, earth, to the shades shall give birth.

text.NRSV: Come, my people, enter your chambers, and shut your doors behind you; hide yourselves for a little while until the wrath is past.
text.Rotherham: Come my people, enter into thy chambers, And shut thy doors behind thee,—Hide thee as it were a little moment Till the indignation pass over.

text.NRSV: For the Lord is coming out of his place to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their iniquity; the earth will disclose the blood shed on it, and will no longer cover its slain.
text.Rotherham: For lo! Yahweh, is coming forth out of his place, To visit the iniquity of earth’s inhabitant upon him,—Therefore shall the earth unveil her shed-blood, And throw a covering, no longer over her slain.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord with his cruel and great and strong sword will punish Leviathan the fleeing serpent, Leviathan the twisting serpent, and he will kill the dragon that is in the sea.
text.Rotherham: In that day, will Yahweh With his sword—the hard and the great and the strong, Bring punishment Upon Leviathan, the fleeing serpent, And upon Leviathan, the crooked serpent,—And will slay the monster which is in the sea.

text.NRSV: On that day: A pleasant vineyard, sing about it!
text.Rotherham: In that day, A Wine-Vineyard! sing ye unto her:

text.NRSV: I, the Lord, am its keeper; every moment I water it. I guard it night and day so that no one can harm it;
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, am Watching over her, Every moment, will I water her,—Lest anyone injure her, Night and day, will I watch over her.

text.NRSV: I have no wrath. If it gives me thorns and briers, I will march to battle against it, I will burn it up altogether.
text.Rotherham: Fury, have I none,—Oh that there were delivered to me briars and thorns, in battle! I would march in among them I would set fire to them one and all.

text.NRSV: Or else let it cling to me for protection, let it make peace with me, let it make peace with me.
text.Rotherham: Else, let one lay hold of my protection, Let him make peace with me,—Peace, let him make with me.

text.NRSV: In days to come Jacob shall take root, Israel shall blossom and put forth shoots, and fill the whole world with fruit.
text.Rotherham: In coming times, shall Jacob strike root, Israel, shall blossom and bud,—Then shall they fill the face of the world with fruit.

text.NRSV: Has he struck them down as he struck those who struck them? Or have they been killed as those were killed who killed them?
text.Rotherham: Was it, with the smiting of his smiter, that he smote him? Or as with the slaying of his slayer, was he slain?

text.NRSV: By expulsion, by exile you struggled against them; with his fierce blast he swept them away in the day of the east wind.
text.Rotherham: By driving her away—by dismissing her, wouldest thou contend with her? He removed her by his rough wind in, a day of east wind.

text.NRSV: Therefore by this the guilt of Jacob will be expiated, and this will be the full fruit of the removal of his sin: when he makes all the stones of the altars like chalkstones crushed to pieces, no sacred poles or incense altars will remain standing.
text.Rotherham: Therefore hereby, shall a propitiatory—covering be put over the iniquity of Jacob, And all, this, is the fruit of taking away his sin,—When he maketh all the stones of an altar like chalk-stones that soon crumble, Sacred Stems and Sun Images, shall not arise.

text.NRSV: For the fortified city is solitary, a habitation deserted and forsaken, like the wilderness; the calves graze there, there they lie down, and finish its boughs.
text.Rotherham: For the fortified city, is solitary, The dwelling forsaken and left as a wilderness,—There, shall the calf feed, And, there, lie down And shall consume the branches thereof:

text.NRSV: When its branches are dry, they are broken; women come and make a fire of them. For this is a people without understanding; therefore he who made them will not have compassion on them, he who formed them will show them no favor.
text.Rotherham: When the cut-off boughs thereof are dry, they shall be broken to pieces, Women, coming, are kindling it! For it is not a people of understanding, For this cause, Will he that made him, not have compassion upon him, And, he that formed him, will shew him no favour.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord will thresh from the channel of the Euphrates to the Wadi of Egypt, and you will be gathered one by one, O people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, That Yahweh will beat off his fruit from the stream of the River [="Euphrates"], unto the torrent-valley of Egypt,—And, ye, shall be picked up, one by one O sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: And on that day a great trumpet will be blown, and those who were lost in the land of Assyria and those who were driven out to the land of Egypt will come and worship the Lord on the holy mountain at Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, That there shall be a blowing with a great horn, Then shall come in Such as have wandered in the land of Assyria, And such as have been outcasts in the land of Egypt,—And they shall bow themselves down unto Yahweh In the holy mountain, In Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Ah, the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim, and the fading flower of its glorious beauty, which is on the head of those bloated with rich food, of those overcome with wine!
text.Rotherham: Alas! for the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim, And for his fading wreath of majestic beauty,—Which is on the head of the fertile valley, of them who are overcome with wine.

text.NRSV: See, the Lord has one who is mighty and strong; like a storm of hail, a destroying tempest, like a storm of mighty, overflowing waters, with his hand he will hurl them down to the earth.
text.Rotherham: Lo! My Lord hath one who is, strong and bold, Like a storm of hail, a destroying tempest, Like a storm of mighty waters overflowing, Hath he thrust it down to the earth with force:

text.NRSV: Trampled under foot will be the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim;
text.Rotherham: With the feet shall be trodden down, the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim,

text.NRSV: and the fading flower of its glorious beauty, which is on the head of those bloated with rich food, will be like a first-ripe fig before the summer; whoever sees it, eats it up as soon as it is in hand.
text.Rotherham: So shall his fading wreath of majestic beauty, Which is on the head of the fertile valley, become—Like the first-ripe fig before fruit-harvest, Which when he that looketh upon it seeth while it is yet in his hand, he swalloweth it up.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord of hosts will be a garland of glory, and a diadem of beauty, to the remnant of his people;
text.Rotherham: In that day, will Yahweh of hosts become A crown of beauty, and A diadem of majesty,—Unto the remnant of his people:

text.NRSV: and a spirit of justice to the one who sits in judgment, and strength to those who turn back the battle at the gate.
text.Rotherham: Even a spirit of justice—to him that presideth over justice, And strength to them who would turn back the battle at the gate.

text.NRSV: These also reel with wine and stagger with strong drink; the priest and the prophet reel with strong drink, they are confused with wine, they stagger with strong drink; they err in vision, they stumble in giving judgment.
text.Rotherham: But as for these, With wine, do they reel, and With strong drink, do they stagger,—Priest and prophet, reel with strong drink They are swallowed up through wine They stagger through strong drink, They reel in prophetic vision, They totter in pronouncing judgment.

text.NRSV: All tables are covered with filthy vomit; no place is clean.
text.Rotherham: For, all tables, are full of filthy vomit,—There is no place!

text.NRSV: “To whom will he teach knowledge, and to whom will he explain the message? Those who are weaned from milk, those taken from the breast?
text.Rotherham: Whom, would he teach knowledge? And, whom, would he cause to understand the message? Them who are weaned from the milk? taken from the breasts?

text.NRSV: For it is precept upon precept, precept upon precept, line upon line, line upon line, here a little, there a little.”
text.Rotherham: For it is—Precept upon precept, precept upon precept, Line upon line, line upon line,—A little here a little there.

text.NRSV: Truly, with stammering lip and with alien tongue he will speak to this people,
text.Rotherham: For, with a jabbering lip, and with an alien tongue, must he speak unto this people!

text.NRSV: to whom he has said, “This is rest; give rest to the weary; and this is repose”; yet they would not hear.
text.Rotherham: To whom he said—This, is the rest—give ye rest to the weary, and This, is the quietness,—But they were unwilling to hear.

text.NRSV: Therefore the word of the Lord will be to them, “Precept upon precept, precept upon precept, line upon line, line upon line, here a little, there a little;” in order that they may go, and fall backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.
